risks: 6 days out -- backup booking window is now critically short; last practical window to act
AirAsia X declared 'a real crisis' April 6 -- raising fares 30-40%, cutting 10% of flights, fuel surcharge up 20%
AirAsia has no fuel hedging; Q1 affiliate losses exceeding $180M; 'manageable but depends on duration'
Thai AirAsia cutting frequencies to 13 destinations; daily disruption pattern ongoing
Vietnam fuel shortage persists -- imports 70% of jet fuel, 60%+ from Thailand and China (both export bans still in effect)
Vietnamese airline cuts active since April 1: Vietnam Airlines suspended 7 domestic routes, VietJet cut 8% regionally
April 13 is Songkran festival start (Apr 13-15) -- DMK airport will be heavily congested on arrival
Trump threatening to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants (April 5 CNBC) -- rhetoric escalating, not de-escalating
mitigations: Short-haul route (2hr, 956km) -- limited fuel exposure per flight vs long-haul
Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK (FD647) daily -- no cancellation announced
SEA airspace completely unaffected by Middle East closures
Brent down slightly to $109.77 (April 6) from $112.42 -- minor fuel cost relief
April 13 departure gives 7-day buffer before Vietnamese visa expiry (April 20)

risks: Middle East airspace closures fully active: Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Syria FIRs all closed by NOTAM
EASA advisory expires April 10 -- 3 days away; renewal almost certain given no ceasefire
Rerouting adds 90 min to 3 hours flight time via southern or northern corridor
Trump threatening further strikes on Iran infrastructure (April 5) -- escalation risk remains live
Strait of Hormuz effectively closed; physical oil supply crisis unresolved
Thai Airways exited bankruptcy only June 2025 -- still in recovery phase with mixed financials
Thai Airways raised ticket prices 10-15%; further surcharges possible
1,000+ cumulative flight cancellations in Thailand due to Gulf crisis (AeroThai data)
20 days to departure -- alternatives window still open but narrowing
mitigations: Thai Airways is government-backed flag carrier -- most resilient carrier on this route
BKK-CPH direct route still operating with rerouting -- no cancellation reported
Southern route (Egypt-Saudi-Oman corridor) established and functioning
Bangkok confirmed April 13-27: two Airbnb bookings in place
Brent slightly down to $109.77 -- minor cost pressure relief
20 days to departure -- some possibility of de-escalation before April 27

fuelOil.summary: Brent crude eased slightly to $109.77/bbl on April 6, down from $112.42. IATA global jet fuel average $195.19/bbl (down 0.9%) -- marginal cooling from $234 peak but still catastrophically elevated vs pre-war $88 baseline. US retail jet fuel at $4.88/gallon (~$205/bbl) as of early April. SEA hub prices estimated ~$215-220/bbl due to regional export bans.
details: Brent crude futures at $109.77/bbl on April 6 (Fortune) -- down $2.65 from $112.42 on April 3-5
IATA global jet fuel average: $195.19/bbl, down 0.9% from prior week (latest monitor, April 6)
US retail jet fuel: $4.88/gallon (~$205/bbl) as of early April (FinancialContent/MarketMinute)
Brent spot (physical cargo) hit $141.36 on April 2 -- highest since 2008 financial crisis
Futures-to-spot premium reflects extreme physical supply tightness from Strait of Hormuz disruption
Jet fuel estimated ~$215-220/bbl at SEA hubs due to regional export bans (Thailand, China both in effect)
Pre-war IATA projection was $88/bbl for 2026 -- current prices are 120%+ above baseline
AirAsia X raised fuel surcharge 20% and average fares 30-40% citing 'real crisis' (Flight Global, April 6)
Vietnam fuel delivery timelines still extended; importing 70% of jet fuel needs
China and Thailand fuel export bans still in effect -- no lifting announced
SAS cancelled 1,000 flights in April 2026 citing fuel crisis
Oil market backwardation: $141 spot vs ~$110 futures = ~$31 premium for immediate physical supply

geopolitical.iranConflict: Active conflict with escalating rhetoric. Trump threatened to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants (CNBC April 5). Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted. Kuwait airport struck by Iranian drones April 1. No ceasefire or diplomatic agreement. Airspace closures fully in effect across Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Syria.
airspaceClosures: Iran (OIIX FIR) -- CLOSED to commercial aviation
Kuwait airspace -- CLOSED (airport struck by Iranian drones April 1)
Israel (LLLL FIR) -- RESTRICTED, limited PPR only
Bahrain, UAE, Qatar -- HEAVILY RESTRICTED, short-notice full closures possible
EASA advisory expires April 10 -- renewal expected given no ceasefire
geopolitical.regionalStability: Southeast Asia airspace stable and unaffected by direct conflict. Fuel supply chains severely disrupted. Vietnam, Thailand, Philippines all facing rationing or export bans. Trump rhetoric escalating April 5 (bombing threats). Kuwait airport drone strike April 1 confirms active conflict. No de-escalation signals as of April 7.
details: Trump reiterates threat to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants (CNBC April 5 2026)
Kuwait International Airport struck by Iranian drone fire April 1 -- large fire at fuel storage tanks
Strait of Hormuz: effective closure for most Western-flagged vessels since Feb 28; unlikely to resolve in 2026
2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis Wikipedia page active -- event recognized as major geopolitical crisis
No formal ceasefire or diplomatic framework exists
EASA advisory expires April 10 -- 3 days from now; renewal watch critical
Traditional Gulf corridor unavailable; rerouting via southern or northern corridors continues
AeroThai cumulative 1,000+ flights cancelled in Thailand alone due to Gulf crisis
Bangkok-Copenhagen adds 90min-3hrs due to mandatory rerouting
Rescue.org warns of 'triple emergency' in humanitarian contexts due to Strait of Hormuz closure

airasia.cancellationRate: Elevated. 116 AirAsia group delays recorded April 6-7. Thai AirAsia cutting frequencies to 13 destinations. 10% of AirAsia X flights cut. Daily disruption pattern ongoing.
airasia.financialHealth: AirAsia X no fuel hedging. Q1 affiliate losses exceeding $180M. AirAsia X raised fares 30-40% and fuel surcharge 20% as of April 6. Described situation as 'a real crisis' (FlightGlobal). FY2025 pre-conflict recovery reversed by fuel crisis. 'Manageable but depends on duration.'
airasia.recentDisruptions: Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK daily (FD647) as of latest checks. Thai AirAsia cutting frequencies to 13 destinations including South Asia, Phnom Penh, Seoul. AirAsia X cut 10% of flights overall. Philippines AirAsia Manila-HCMC suspended through May. AirAsia X described crisis as most severe since COVID.

thaiAirways.cancellationRate: Near normal for rerouted operations. Individual Bangkok route cancellations reported (THA407 to SIN). BKK-CPH operating with rerouting.
thaiAirways.financialHealth: Exited bankruptcy June 2025 as privatized public company. Mixed financials: net loss full year, quarterly profit in recent quarter. Government-backed. 10-15% ticket price increases to absorb fuel costs. Brent at ~$110 maintains cost pressure.
thaiAirways.recentDisruptions: BKK-CPH operating with rerouting via southern or northern corridor. Adds 90 min-3hrs flight time. No BKK-CPH cancellations reported. London-Bangkok capacity added due to Gulf corridor stranded passengers. Thai Airways adjusting routes while maintaining European schedule.

cxrToDmk.notes: SEA airspace unaffected by Middle East closures. Primary risk: Vietnam jet fuel shortage and AirAsia operational reliability at 6 days out. FD647 still operating but verify in AirAsia app today (April 7). Songkran (April 13-15) starts on flight day -- DMK will be at peak congestion. AirAsia X 10% capacity cuts announced April 6 -- check if FD647 is in scope. Backup: VietJet or Vietnam Airlines CXR-BKK same day.

bkkToCph.notes: Traditional Gulf corridor unavailable. Thai Airways rerouting via southern (Egypt-Saudi-Oman, +90min) or northern (Caucasus/Central Asia, +2-3hrs) corridors. EASA advisory expires April 10 -- watch for renewal in 3 days. Escalating Trump rhetoric (bombing threats April 5) reduces chance of April 10 de-escalation. Route operating but delays expected. Expect 13-15hr flight instead of 11-12hr.

actions.action: Open AirAsia app NOW and verify FD647 CXR-DMK April 13 status. AirAsia X just cut 10% of flights (April 6 announcement) -- confirm FD647 is not affected. If any disruption signal: book VietJet or Vietnam Airlines CXR-BKK same day immediately. 6 days out is last viable window.

Check safeairspace.net or ops.group on April 10 for EASA advisory renewal. If extended past April 10: BKK-CPH rerouting confirmed through April 27, expect 13-15hr flight. If narrowed or lifted: Thai Airways routing may improve. Also confirms whether Brent will hold or spike.
Confirm Thai Airways BKK-CPH April 27 booking is active and has no schedule changes due to rerouting. Check thaiairways.com booking reference. Note: expect 90min-3hrs longer than booked time.

Track Brent crude through April 13. Currently at $109.77 -- slight relief. If it climbs back toward $120+, upgrade AirAsia flight to RED and consider backup options. Check oilprice.com or Fortune daily.

oilPriceTimeline.event: Pre-conflict baseline. Markets calm, IATA forecast $88/bbl for 2026.
Brent drops to $67.4 as US-Iran diplomatic talks ease tensions.
Markets turn bullish anticipating US military action against Iran. Brent trading ~$10 above fair value.
Last day before strikes. Oil at pre-war levels.
US-Israeli strikes on Iranian military targets. War begins. Immediate price spike.
Iranian missile/drone retaliation. Brent surges 10-13% from pre-war.
Iranian drones strike Nakhchivan airport (Azerbaijan). Brent up 15% since war began.
Thailand bans fuel exports. China restricts refined oil exports.
Peak spike. Near halt in Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic. 65% increase from Feb 27.
Partial correction. Still up 37% from pre-war. IATA warns of sudden price challenge.
IATA weekly monitor: jet fuel $197/bbl for week ending Mar 20, up 58.4% from Feb 20.
Brent eases but jet fuel stays elevated due to supply chain lag.
Iran rejects US peace talks. Prices reignite. Jet fuel up 12.6% week-on-week.
Brent closes at highest since July 2022, up 4.2% on day.
Brent at 4-year highs. Jet fuel peaks at $234.3/bbl. Vietnamese airline cuts take effect April 1. Goldman Sachs: $14-18 geopolitical risk premium.
SHARP REVERSAL. Trump says war could end in 2-3 weeks. Brent drops $12/bbl (-10.3%). Spot Brent hits $141.36 -- highest since 2008. Kuwait airport struck by Iranian drones.
DE-ESCALATION FAILS TO HOLD. Brent reverses from $101 back to $112.42 (+11.2%). Trump signal not sustained. Physical supply crisis remains acute. IATA jet fuel avg: $195.19 (down 0.9%).

Slight easing. Brent dips to $109.77 (-2.4% from Apr 3-5). IATA jet fuel $195/bbl. But Trump threatens to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants -- escalatory rhetoric continues. AirAsia X declares 'real crisis', raises fares 30-40%, cuts 10% of flights.

cancellationTimeline.details: Normal disruption levels across SEA. Typical daily: 50-100 delays, <20 cancellations region-wide.
1,198 delays + 21 cancellations across Changi, Jakarta, Bali, Manila, Cebu. Pre-war disruptions.
4,216 delays + 62 cancellations. AirAsia alone had 247 delays. Infrastructure strain at SEA hubs.
Disruptions across Thailand, Japan, Indonesia, Vietnam, Philippines, Hong Kong.
1,400+ delays + 93 cancellations. Jakarta: 104 delays, KL: 470 delays.
WAR BEGINS. 2,513 cancellations + 2,117 delays. Changi: 32 cancelled. BKK: 32 cancelled. Massive wave.
150-300% above normal daily disruption rates. JAL, Thai Airways, Air India, ANA, AirAsia, Lion Air all affected.
328 Thailand flights disrupted. Hundreds of passengers stranded across Asia.
1,043 delays + 184 cancellations across SEA. AirAsia among hardest-hit carriers.
AeroThai reports cumulative 1,000+ flights cancelled in Thailand alone due to Gulf crisis.
65+ international/regional flights cancelled across Indonesia, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Taiwan.
325 delays + 50 cancellations in Thailand. Thai Airways, Bangkok Airways, AirAsia, Emirates affected.
Vietnamese airlines announce coordinated pullback from April 1. Vietnam Airlines suspending 7 domestic routes, cutting 23 weekly flights. VietJet cutting 8%.
Vietnamese airline cuts NOW ACTIVE. 5,993 delays + 278 cancellations across 19 Asian airports. Thai AirAsia alone: 161 delays at DMK. AirAsia group: 191 delays. Kuwait airport drone strike.

5,993 delays + 278 cancellations logged April 3 across 19 airports. Thai AirAsia frequency cuts to 13 destinations confirmed. AirAsia X cancelled daily DMK-Shanghai Pudong. Philippines AirAsia Manila-HCMC suspended through May. SAS cancelled 1,000 European flights.

AirAsia: 116 group delays recorded. AirAsia X officially announces 10% flight cuts and 30-40% fare increases (April 6). Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK (FD647). Pattern unchanged. Key watch: EASA advisory expiry April 10.

seaAirlineSummary: Marginal improvement in oil price (Brent $109.77, down from $112.42) but AirAsia X escalated its crisis response April 6: raising fares 30-40%, cutting 10% of flights, calling it 'a real crisis'. Trump threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure April 5 -- escalatory rhetoric persists. EASA advisory expires April 10 (3 days). Vietnamese airline cuts still active. AirAsia CXR-DMK (FD647) still operating. Thai Airways BKK-CPH still operating with rerouting.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Vietnam Airlines
seaAirlineOverview.impact: April 1 cuts active: 7 domestic routes suspended, 23 weekly flights cut. Partial restoration projected April 10-15 contingent on fuel shipments arriving.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Imports 70% of jet fuel, 60%+ from Thailand and China, both maintaining export bans. Fuel delivery timelines still extended at 50+ days.

seaAirlineOverview.name: VietJet Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: 8% regional network reduction active since April 1. Korea-Vietnam routes cancelled including Nha Trang flights.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Budget model highly vulnerable to elevated fuel costs. Brent at ~$110 keeps margins under severe pressure.

seaAirlineOverview.name: AirAsia (Group)
seaAirlineOverview.impact: AirAsia X: 10% flight cuts, fares up 30-40%, fuel surcharge up 20% (announced April 6). 116 group delays recorded April 6-7. Thai AirAsia cutting frequencies to 13 destinations. Philippines AirAsia Manila-HCMC suspended through May.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: No fuel hedging. AirAsia X called this 'a real crisis' (FlightGlobal April 6). Raised fares most since COVID. Thai AirAsia still operating CXR-DMK daily (FD647) as of April 7.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Thai Airways
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Rerouting European services via southern or northern corridor. BKK-CPH still operating. Raised prices 10-15%. Individual Bangkok routes showing cancellations.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Exited bankruptcy June 2025. Government-backed but in recovery phase. Fuel cost pressure maintained with Brent at ~$110.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Cebu Pacific
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Major route suspensions: Davao-BKK, Iloilo-BKK, Iloilo-SGN, Clark-HAN all suspended Apr-Oct 2026.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Philippines fuel emergency still in effect. Among most aggressive carriers in cutting routes.

seaAirlineOverview.name: SAS
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Cancelled 1,000 flights in April 2026 citing fuel crisis. Focusing on short-haul routes within Norway.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: European carriers responding aggressively to fuel cost spike. Relevant context for CPH arrivals.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Bangkok Airways
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Fuel surcharges of 1,200-2,000 baht. No major route suspensions reported.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Boutique positioning helps retain premium passengers. Ongoing fuel cost pressure.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Nok Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Fuel surcharges in effect. Domestic Thai routes under pressure.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Low-cost model. Operating on thin margins.

seaAirlineOverview.name: Lion Air
seaAirlineOverview.impact: Part of broader disruption pattern. Indonesian domestic market under fuel pressure.
seaAirlineOverview.detail: Indonesian carriers facing similar constraints. Limited specific recent data.

broaderContext.narrative: April 7 update: marginal Brent improvement ($109.77 vs $112.42) is offset by AirAsia X escalating its crisis posture -- raising fares 30-40% and cutting 10% of flights, calling the situation 'a real crisis' in FlightGlobal April 6. Trump's April 5 threat to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants signals no diplomatic wind-down is imminent. EASA advisory expires in 3 days (April 10) -- watch for renewal. Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted. Physical oil markets still in extreme backwardation. The situation is stable but at a persistently high stress level. No de-escalation has materialized.

policies.description: PM Order 2/2026: Banned exports of gasoline, gasohol, diesel, Jet A-1 aviation fuel, and LPG (except to Cambodia and Laos). Froze diesel prices. Required large fuel traders to increase reserves. Still in effect.
Banned refined fuel exports, including jet fuel. Directly cut off ~60% of Vietnam's jet fuel supply. Still in effect.
Declared fuel emergency. 4-day government work week. Philippine carriers among most aggressive in cutting routes.

Coordinated airline pullback in effect. Vietnam Airlines: 7 domestic routes suspended, 23 weekly flights cut. VietJet: 8% regional reduction. Partial restoration projected April 10-15 contingent on fuel shipments.
Military junta introduced odds-and-evens driving system (fuel rationing).
Trump threatens to bomb every Iranian bridge and power plant (CNBC). Escalatory rhetoric after April 1 de-escalation signal failed. No formal ceasefire or agreement.

broaderContext.outlook: Stable but elevated stress. No de-escalation has materialized. Key watch dates: April 10 (EASA advisory renewal -- critical for BKK-CPH), April 13 (AirAsia CXR-DMK + Songkran peak -- 6 days out, verify booking today), April 15 (Vietnam fuel restoration window), April 27 (Thai Airways BKK-CPH). If EASA extends past April 10, BKK-CPH rerouting is confirmed for full duration. If Brent climbs to $125+, budget airline sustainability becomes critical.
